scenario: Iran counter-proposal via Pakistan May 10: Lebanon ceasefire + Iranian oil exports first; nuclear/Hormuz deferred to 30-day follow-on (Trump: 'unacceptable'/'playing games'). Blockade 70+ tankers; IEA: 14M bpd Gulf cut (Iraq, Saudi, Kuwait, UAE, Qatar, Bahrain) depleting global inventories at record pace. Brent ~$106 May 15 (TradingEconomics est.; peaked $110.87 D72 Fortune confirmed); TTF €47.61 (D73 confirmed +1.47%); JKM $16.99 (D71 confirmed); Hormuz ~90% closed; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; tail 40%.

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat firms (May 15 est.; Brent ~$106 +0.2%); Iran MOU counter-proposal stalled; Qafco force majeure and Cape rerouting disruption unchanged; fertilizer supply mechanism intact; blockade 70+ tankers delays resolution. Tail 40%. Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel correlation up with Brent ~$106 (May 15 est.); Iran MOU stalled; Cape routing disruption unchanged; blockade 70+ tankers; IEA record inventory depletion; tail 40%. Fertilizer disruption for Q2 planting unresolved.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker costs elevated with Brent ~$106 (May 15 est.); Iran MOU counter-proposal stalled; Cape routing costs persist; blockade 70+ tankers; IEA 14M bpd Gulf cut at record pace; tail 40%.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO firms with Brent ~$106 (May 15 est.); Iran MOU stalled; MOU sequencing gap delays Hormuz reopening timeline; tail 40%. Indonesia B40 mandate structural floor intact. Malaysia June supply gap unresolved. Fertilizer disruption for Q2 planting unresolved. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD (est.).

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security pressure persists; Brent ~$106 (May 15 est.); Iran MOU counter-proposal stalled; Qafco force majeure and Q2 planting urea disruption unresolved; blockade 70+ tankers; IEA record depletion; tail 40%. Malaysia June supply gap risk remains.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha firms with Brent ~$106 (May 15 est.); Gulf condensate interdiction unchanged; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; Pengerang RAPID feedstock exposure elevated; Iran MOU stalled; IEA 14M bpd Gulf cut; tail 40%. OFAC Economic Fury designations (May 11) tighten Iranian condensate enforcement. Source: ICIS/Platts (est.).

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene tracks naphtha (May 15 est.); SABIC/QAPCO shipping bottlenecks persist; Iran MOU counter-proposal stalled; vessel interdiction not being lifted; blockade 70+ tankers; tail 40%. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol eased from D72 Brent-peak levels (May 15 est.; Brent ~$106); US Iran sanctions waiver expired Apr 19; Qatar force majeure unresolved; OFAC Economic Fury (May 11) tightens IRGC petrochemical enforcement; MOU stalled; tail 40%. China MTO complexes: feedstock pressure moderately elevated. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea firms with Brent ~$106 (May 15 est.); Qafco force majeure persists; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; Iran MOU stalled; blockade 70+ tankers. US sanctions waiver expiry (Apr 19) + OFAC Economic Fury (May 11) tightens Iranian-origin enforcement. Tail 40%. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP firms (May 15 est.; Brent ~$106); Saudi Ma'aden shipping disruption unresolved; US Gulf supply stretched; blockade 70+ tankers; Iran MOU counter-proposal stalled; IEA record depletion. Wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam acute; tail 40%.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight ticks with Brent ~$106 (May 15 est.); less directly exposed to Hormuz than urea or DAP; Cape rerouting premium persists; Iran MOU stalled - outlook delayed; palm oil plantations facing moderately elevated input costs; tail 40%.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI firms (May 15 est.); IEA 14M bpd Gulf cut at record pace supports dry bulk premium; P&I suspension persists; blockade 70+ tankers; Iran MOU stalled - mine-clearing 6 months even on deal. Hormuz reopening normalization pushed further out. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates firm on MOU stalemate (May 15 est.; Brent ~$106); Iran MOU counter-proposal unresolved; blockade 70+ tankers maintains Hormuz risk premium; Trump-Xi (May 14) partial relief was temporary; tail (40%) would push rates to cycle highs. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: SCFI carried (weekly; last published Friday May 8). Cape rerouting premium persists - Iran MOU stalled delays normalization; blockade 70+ tankers; MSC Francesca still seized; P&I unresolved. Asia-Europe normalization requires Hormuz reopening + P&I restoration + mine-clearing (~6 months); tail 40%. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (weekly).
